title: Count All Objects in Combined Sets
description: Count every object across two or more visible groups to determine the total number of objects.
condition: Given two or more visible groups of objects and asked for the total number of objects.
response: Count each object once and state the total.
label: Production / Skill
example: Count 3 squares and 4 trapezoids to get $7$ total shapes.
notes: Requires one-to-one correspondence and keeping track of counted objects.

title: Interpret Add-To, Result Unknown Story
description: Recognize that a story describing an initial amount and more being added requires addition to find the result.
condition: A story problem states an initial quantity and that more are added, asking how many in all.
response: Represent the situation with addition and find the total.
label: Rule / Plan
example: There are 3 stickers and 2 more are added: compute $3 + 2 = 5$.
notes: Focuses on structure of Add To, Result Unknown problems.

title: Interpret Minus as Take Away
description: Understand that the minus sign indicates removing a quantity from a starting amount.
condition: Given an expression a − b or a story where some objects are taken away.
response: Subtract b from a to find how many remain.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Interpret Plus as Put Together
description: Understand that the plus sign indicates combining two quantities.
condition: Given an expression a + b or a picture showing two groups being combined.
response: Combine the quantities and find the total.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Supports matching pictures to addition expressions.

title: Interpret Take-From, Result Unknown Story
description: Recognize that a story describing a starting amount and some leaving requires subtraction to find the result.
condition: A story problem states a starting quantity and that some are taken away, asking how many remain.
response: Represent the situation with subtraction and find the difference.
label: Rule / Plan
example: There are 6 kids and 2 leave: compute $6 - 2 = 4$.
notes: Focuses on structure of Take From, Result Unknown problems.

title: Match Picture Representation to Expression
description: Identify which numerical expression corresponds to a visual representation of quantities being added or subtracted.
condition: Given a picture showing quantities combined or taken away and a set of expressions.
response: Select or match the expression that represents the picture.
label: Rule / Plan
example: A picture of 5 circles with 2 crossed out matches $5 - 2$.
notes: Requires interpreting both quantity and operation.

title: Use Identity Property of Addition with Zero
description: Recognize that adding zero to a number results in the same number.
condition: Given an addition expression of the form a + 0 or 0 + a.
response: State that the sum equals a.
label: Principle / Model
notes: resp_type marked Constant because the response preserves the original number a.
